Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given an event log of an insurance claims processing process The steps are Submit Claim Evaluate Claim Request Additional Documents Approve Claim Pay Claim Close Claim Each event has a timestamp resource region and complexity We need to identify cases with performance issues long durations and analyze how attributes Resource Region Complexity correlate with longer lead times

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets compute the duration for each case The duration is from the first event Submit Claim to the last event Close Claim for each case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 Submit Claim 2024 04 01 09 00 Close Claim 2024 04 01 10 30 Duration 1 hour 30 minutes 90 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 Submit Claim 2024 04 01 09 05 Close Claim 2024 04 02 11 00 Duration 1 day 1 hour 55 minutes More precisely from 09 05 on Apr 1 to 11 00 on Apr 2 is 1 day plus 11 00 minus 09 05 equals 24 hours plus 1 hour 55 min equals 25 hours 55 min or about 1555 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 Submit Claim 2024 04 01 09 10 Close Claim 2024 04 03 09 30 Duration 2 days 20 minutes Actually from Apr 1 09 10 to Apr 3 09 30 is 2 days plus 20 minutes equals 48 hours 20 min equals 2900 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 Submit Claim 2024 04 01 09 20 Close Claim 2024 04 01 10 45 Duration 1 hour 25 minutes 85 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 Submit Claim 2024 04 01 09 25 Close Claim 2024 04 04 14 30 Duration 3 days 5 hours 5 minutes From Apr 1 09 25 to Apr 4 14 30 Thats 3 days plus 14 30 minus 09 25 equals 5 hours 5 minutes So total 72 hours plus 5 hours 5 min equals 77 hours 5 min equals 4625 minutes

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So clearly cases 2002 2003 and 2005 have significantly longer durations compared to 2001 and 2004 Case 2005 is the longest then 2003 then 2002

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now we need to analyze the attributes Resource Region Complexity

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 2001 Complexity Low Region A Resources CSR Jane Adjuster Mike Manager Ann Finance Alan CSR Jane Duration short Case 2002 Complexity Medium Region B Resources CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa Manager Ann Finance Carl CSR Paul Duration medium about 1 day Case 2003 Complexity High Region A Resources CSR Jane Adjuster Mike Manager Bill Finance Alan CSR Jane Duration long over 2 days Case 2004 Complexity Low Region B Resources CSR Mary Adjuster Lisa Manager Ann Finance Carl CSR Mary Duration short Case 2005 Complexity High Region B Resources CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa Manager Bill Finance Carl CSR Paul Duration very long over 3 days

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity seems to play a role High complexity cases 2003 2005 take longer Medium complexity 2002 takes longer than low complexity 2001 2004 But note 2002 is medium and it took about a day while low took less than 2 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region A has cases 2001 low short and 2003 high long Region B has 2002 medium medium 2004 low short 2005 high very long So both regions have short and long cases so region alone may not be the cause However note that in Region B the high complexity case 2005 took longer than the high complexity case in Region A 2003 So maybe region B has issues with high complexity cases But we have only one high complexity case per region so hard to generalize

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Look at the Request Additional Documents activity Cases that have this activity tend to take longer Case 2001 no request short Case 2002 one request medium duration Case 2003 two requests long Case 2004 no request short Case 2005 three requests very long So the number of Request Additional Documents events correlates with duration

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But why do some cases have more requests Possibly due to complexity High complexity cases have more requests 2003 High had 2 requests 2005 High had 3 requests 2002 Medium had 1 request 2001 and 2004 Low had 0 requests So complexity likely influences the need for additional documents

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In case 2003 the first request is at 11 00 on Apr 1 second at 17 00 on Apr 1 then approval on Apr 2 16 00 There is a gap of almost a day between the second request and approval In case 2005 there are three requests Apr 1 11 30 Apr 2 17 00 Apr 3 15 00 then approval on Apr 4 10 00 There are gaps between requests and next steps

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Possibly after requesting documents there is waiting time for the claimant to respond But the log does not show when documents are received it only shows the requests So the delay might be due to waiting for documents

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: However note that in case 2002 there is a request at 14 00 on Apr 1 and then approval the next day at 10 00 so maybe overnight In case 2003 the second request is at 17 00 on Apr 1 and approval is at 16 00 on Apr 2 which is about 23 hours later In case 2005 the third request is at 15 00 on Apr 3 and approval at 10 00 on Apr 4 about 19 hours later So there seems to be a pattern after a request the next activity either another request or approval happens the next day

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Possibly the process is designed such that after requesting documents they wait for a day before checking if documents arrived Or maybe the adjuster works only during certain hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In case 2003 there are two requests on the same day one at 11 00 and one at 17 00 So the adjuster can do multiple requests in a day if needed But then after the second request there is a delay until the next days afternoon

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So maybe the approval step requires manager involvement and managers might only review once a day Or maybe the claim is put in a queue and processed batch wise

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Notice that cases with Manager Bill 2003 and 2005 took longer than those with Manager Ann Maybe Manager Bill is slower in approval But in 2003 approval happened on Apr 2 16 00 after multiple requests In 2005 approval on Apr 4 10 00 So maybe Manager Bill has a delay in approving But also the requests for additional documents might be causing delays

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets compare approval times relative to the previous event For case 2001 Previous event Evaluate Claim at 09 30 Approval at 10 00 30 minutes For case 2002 Previous event Request Additional Documents at 14 00 on Apr 1 Approval at 10 00 on Apr 2 about 20 hours later For case 2003 Previous event second Request Additional Documents at 17 00 on Apr 1 Approval at 16 00 on Apr 2 about 23 hours For case 2004 Previous event Evaluate Claim at 09 35 Approval at 10 00 25 minutes For case 2005 Previous event third Request Additional Documents at 15 00 on Apr 3 Approval at 10 00 on Apr 4 about 19 hours

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the approval step after a request has a delay of about a day This could be because the manager only checks once a day or because the documents are not yet submitted Without knowing when documents are submitted we cannot tell

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But note that in case 2003 there are two requests on the same day Possibly after the first request the adjuster might not have received documents and sent a reminder That could explain the second request Then after the second request they wait for documents So the delay might be due to the claimants response time

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So one root cause High complexity claims often require additional documents and the process of requesting and waiting for documents introduces delays Moreover multiple rounds of requests exacerbate the delay

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In case 2005 there are three requests and the approval happens the day after the third request In case 2003 two requests approval the day after the second request So the number of requests directly adds days

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Maybe the root cause is that for high complexity claims the process requires multiple document checks and each cycle of request wait review takes about a day So high complexity claims inherently take longer

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Suggestions to mitigate For high complexity claims perhaps assign more experienced adjusters who can request all necessary documents at once reducing multiple rounds Implement a system to track document submission and set reminders to claimants to submit documents faster Maybe streamline the approval process for high complexity claims by having managers review claims immediately after documents are received rather than waiting for a daily batch

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: To answer the task Cases 2002 2003 and 2005 are taking longer Specifically 2005 is the longest then 2003 then 2002

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity High complexity cases 2003 2005 have longer durations Medium complexity 2002 also longer than low The number of Request Additional Documents activities increases with complexity Low 0 Medium 1 High 2 or 3 This suggests that complexity leads to more document requests which introduce waiting times

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Resource Adjuster Lisa handled the case with the most requests 2005 and also handled a medium case with one request Adjuster Mike handled a high complexity case with two requests So both adjusters handle high complexity but Lisa had three requests for one high complexity case Maybe Lisa is more thorough or maybe the case required more documents Manager Bill handled the approvals for the high complexity cases and these approvals happened after multiple requests Manager Ann handled approvals for low and medium cases which were quicker So Manager Bill might be associated with longer approval cycles but it could be because he handles more complex cases that already have delays

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region Both regions have short and long cases but region Bs high complexity case took longer than region As high complexity case Possibly due to resource differences or claim specifics Since Manager Bill appears in both regions region might not be a direct cause

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Explanations High complexity claims require more information leading to multiple rounds of document requests Each request response cycle introduces waiting time often overnight or longer The approval step seems to occur only after all documents are received and there might be a delay in the managers approval after document receipt

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Suggestions implement a checklist during initial evaluation to request all necessary documents at once reducing multiple rounds Use automated reminders to claimants to submit documents promptly Consider setting shorter review cycles for pending documents Assign experienced adjusters to high complexity claims Analyze the performance of managers and adjusters to see if some are slower and provide training if needed
